parameters.query: SELECT key, content FROM soul ORDER BY key
SELECT key, content FROM agents ORDER BY key
SELECT name, display_name, timezone, preferences, context, setup_done FROM user_profiles WHERE user_id = '{{ $('Merge Input').first().json.qualifiedUserId }}' LIMIT 1
SELECT role, content FROM conversations WHERE session_id = '{{ $('Merge Input').first().json.sessionId }}' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20
SELECT server_name, path, mcp_url, description, tools FROM mcp_registry WHERE active = true ORDER BY server_name

INSERT INTO conversations (session_id, user_id, role, content) VALUES ('{{ $json.sessionId }}', '{{ $json.userId }}', 'user', '{{ $json.userMessage.replace(/'/g, "''").replace(/\$/g, '$$') }}'); INSERT INTO conversations (session_id, user_id, role, content) VALUES ('{{ $json.sessionId }}', '{{ $json.userId }}', 'assistant', '{{ $json.assistantMessage.replace(/'/g, "''").replace(/\$/g, '$$') }}'); {{ $json.markSetupDone ? "UPDATE user_profiles SET setup_done = true WHERE user_id = '" + $json.qualifiedUserId + "';" : '' }}

INSERT INTO memory_daily (date, content, role, user_id) VALUES (CURRENT_DATE, '{{ ($json.userMessage + ' → ' + $json.assistantMessage).replace(/'/g, "''").replace(/\$/g, '$$') }}', 'interaction', '{{ $json.userId }}')

parameters.description:
Search long-term memory. Input: search query string OR JSON with optional filters.
Simple: "Berlin restaurants" — semantic search.
Filtered: {"query": "restaurants", "entity": "Berlin", "tags": ["food"]}
Returns: id, content, category, importance, similarity, tags, entity_name, source.
Use the returned id for memory_update or memory_delete.

Save important info to long-term memory. Input: JSON with content (string), category (general/decision/preference/contact/project), importance (1-10). Optional: tags (string array of keywords), entity_name (string — person/company/place name), source (conversation/user_stated/task_result).

Delete a memory entry by ID. Input: JSON with id (number, required). Always search memory first to get the ID before deleting. Use when information is wrong, obsolete, or the user explicitly asks to forget something.

Update an existing memory entry by ID. Input: JSON with id (number, required), and optional fields to update: content (string), category (general/decision/preference/contact/project), importance (1-10), tags (string array), entity_name (string), source (string). Always search memory first to get the ID before updating.

Manage the knowledge graph — entities (people, companies, projects, places, events, topics) and their relationships.

Input: JSON with action and parameters.

Actions:
- search: Find entities. Optional: query (semantic search), name (text match), type (free text filter, e.g. person/company/project)
- save: Create entity. Required: name. Optional: entity_type (free text — person, company, project, place, event, topic, product, skill, concept, or anything fitting), summary, metadata
- update: Update entity. Required: id (UUID). Optional: name, entity_type, summary, metadata
- relate: Create relationship. Required: source_name, target_name, relation_type (free text — use any descriptive verb/phrase like works_at, knows, interested_in, recommended, collaborated_on, lives_in, etc.). Optional: weight (0-1), valid_from, valid_until
- graph: Get entity network. Required: name. Optional: depth (default 2)
- delete: Remove entity and its relations. Required: id (UUID)

Examples:
{"action":"save","name":"Bastian Hiller","entity_type":"person","summary":"Speaker at DestinationCamp 2026"}
{"action":"relate","source_name":"Bastian Hiller","target_name":"DestinationCamp","relation_type":"speaks_at"}
{"action":"graph","name":"DestinationCamp","depth":2}
{"action":"search","query":"tourism conference","type":"event"}
Make HTTP requests. Input: JSON with url (string) and optional method (GET/POST).
Search the web. Input: search query as string or JSON with q field. Returns top 5 results with title, URL, and snippet.

Read a webpage and return its content as clean markdown. Input: URL string or JSON with url field. Use this instead of HTTP Tool when you need to read webpage content (articles, docs, blog posts). Returns clean text without HTML boilerplate.
List or manage n8n workflows. Input: JSON with action (list/get/create) and optional workflowId.

Set a timed reminder, scheduled task, or recurring action. Modes:
- Reminder (type='reminder'): sends a Telegram message at the given time. Use for 'remind me at/in...'.
- Task (type='task'): the agent executes the instructions once at the given time and sends the result. Use for 'do X at Y o'clock'.
- Recurring (type='recurring'): creates a repeating scheduled action. Extra fields: name (label), instruction (what to do), schedule (object), action_type ('agent_task' or 'briefing', default 'agent_task'), notify_mode ('always' or 'on_change', default 'always'), timezone (default 'Europe/Berlin'). Schedule formats: {"type":"interval","minutes":30} or {"type":"daily","time":"08:00"} or {"type":"weekly","days":[1,5],"time":"09:00"} (1=Mon..7=Sun). notify_mode: 'always' = always send result (use for briefings, reports). 'on_change' = only notify when something NEW is found (use for background monitoring like checking emails, news, prices, stock).
- Management (scheduled actions): action='list' (show all scheduled actions), action='enable'/'disable'/'delete' with target_id (ID of the action to manage).
- Management (one-time reminders): action='list_reminders' (show all pending reminders with their IDs), action='edit_reminder' with target_id + message and/or time (update a pending reminder), action='delete_reminder' with target_id (delete a reminder).
Input for reminder/task: time (ISO 8601), message (text), type. Input for recurring: see above. Input for management: action, target_id.

Builds new n8n workflows for general automations and integrations. Use when the user explicitly wants to automate something or build an integration workflow. NOT for MCP servers — use mcp_builder for that. NOT as a fallback when other tools fail. Input: JSON with 'task' (description of the automation to build).

parameters.name: mcp_builder
parameters.description: Builds a new MCP Server workflow in n8n. ALWAYS use this when the user wants to build an MCP server, MCP tool, or API integration as MCP. NEVER use WorkflowBuilder for MCP. On error: report the error, do NOT fall back to WorkflowBuilder. Input: JSON with 'task' (description of what the server should do).

Calls a tool on an MCP server. Use when the user asks something that an available MCP server can answer (weather, book lookup, etc.). Required parameters: mcp_url (server URL), tool_name (tool to call), arguments (JSON object with parameters). Check mcp_registry table for available servers and their tools.
